html:
[1]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - Imperially sanctioned Complete Library of the Four Treasuries – a title referring to the massive imperial Chinese literary compilation commissioned during the Qing dynasty.
  • 子部十二 - Twelfth category of the Zibu (Philosophical and Miscellaneous Works) – a classification in the Four Treasuries catalog, referring to works of philosophy, anecdotes, and miscellaneous writings.
  • 洛阳缙绅旧闻记 - Records of Old Stories from the Gentry of Luoyang – the title of the text being described, indicating a collection of anecdotes and historical notes about the gentry of Luoyang.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - We subjects respectfully note – a conventional formula used in imperial cataloging to introduce a summary or commentary.
  • 累官同中书门下平章事 - Accumulated appointments to the position of Chancellor of the Central Secretariat and the Office of the Door of the Palace – a high-ranking government position in the Song dynasty.
  • 以司空致仕 - Retired as a Grand Works Minister – a title used for a high-ranking official retiring from office.
  • 卒諡文定 - Died and was posthumously honored with the title 'Wen Ding' – 'Wen Ding' is a posthumous title given to a deceased official.
  • 事迹具宋史本传 - His biography is detailed in the official history of the Song dynasty – referring to the Song Shi (History of the Song).
  • 是书前题乙巳岁 - The preface of this book is dated the year of the Earth Snake (乙巳) – a traditional Chinese way of marking the year using the sexagenary cycle.
  • 真宗景徳二年 - The second year of the Jingde era of Emperor Zhenzong – a specific year in the reign of Emperor Zhenzong of the Song dynasty.
  • 兵部尚书知青州 - Served as the Governor of Qingzhou while holding the title of Minister of War – a high-ranking official position.
  • 梁唐以还 - Since the Later Liang and Later Tang dynasties – referring to the Five Dynasties period following the fall of the Tang.
  • 凡二十一篇 - A total of twenty-one essays – indicating the number of chapters or entries in the book.
  • 书録解题目次与此本合 - The catalog of the book's titles matches this edition – a reference to the Four Treasuries catalog.
  • 晁氏读书志 - Chao Shi's Book of Reading Records – a classical bibliography compiled by Chao Shi, a Song dynasty scholar.
  • 自序明言五卷 - The preface clearly states that it is five volumes – a direct reference to the author's own preface.
  • 永乐大典 - The Yongle Dadian – a massive imperial encyclopedic compilation from the Ming dynasty.
  • 读书志之误明矣 - The error in Book of Reading Records is clearly evident – indicating a discrepancy between sources.
  • 书中多据传说之词 - The book contains many accounts based on legends – suggesting that the work relies on oral or traditional stories.
  • 约载事实以为劝诫 - It records facts approximately for the purpose of moral instruction – indicating the didactic purpose of the text.
  • 自称凡与正史差异者并存而录之 - The author himself states that all accounts differing from the official histories are recorded – indicating the author's approach to compiling the text.
  • 别传外传之比 - Comparable to other biographies and external accounts – suggesting that the text is similar in style or purpose to other historical or anecdotal works.
  • 衡阳周令妻报应 - The story of the wife of the magistrate of Hengyang and her karmic retribution – a specific anecdote included in the text.
  • 焦生见亡妻 - Jiao's encounter with his deceased wife – another story included in the text.
  • 不免涉于 - Cannot help but involve – a phrase indicating that the text may have elements that are considered superstitious or fantastical.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钦定四库全书 - The Complete Library of the Four Treasuries (四库全书), a monumental literary project commissioned by the Qianlong Emperor of the Qing dynasty, which aimed to compile and classify all Chinese knowledge.
  • 子部十二 - The twelfth category in the Four Treasuries classification system, which organizes texts into four major categories: Jing (classics), Shi (histories), Zi (philosophical and miscellaneous works), and Ji (collected writings).
  • 洛阳缙绅旧闻记 - A collection of anecdotes and historical notes about the gentry (缙绅) of Luoyang, a historically significant city that was the capital of several dynasties.
  • 杂事之属 - Under the category of miscellaneous events – a sub-classification of 小说家类.
  • 张齐贤 - Zhang Qiyan (942–1014), a prominent Song dynasty official and scholar known for his administrative reforms and literary works.
  • 太平兴国二年进士 - The second year of the Taiping Xingguo era (977) during the reign of Emperor Taizong of the Song dynasty.
  • 同中书门下平章事 - A high-ranking position equivalent to Chancellor of the Central Secretariat and the Office of the Door of the Palace, one of the highest governmental posts in the Song dynasty.
  • 司空 - A title of the Grand Works Minister, one of the Three Dukes (三公) in the Zhou dynasty, later used as a ceremonial title for retired officials.
  • 致仕 - To retire from office, often with honors.
  • 諡文定 - The posthumous title 'Wen Ding' given to Zhang Qiyan, meaning 'cultured and stable.'
  • 宋史本传 - The biography of Zhang Qiyan in the Song Shi, the official history of the Song dynasty.
  • 乙巳岁 - The year of the Earth Snake in the Chinese sexagenary cycle, corresponding to the year 1005.
  • 真宗景徳二年 - The second year of the Jingde era (1005), during the reign of Emperor Zhenzong of the Song dynasty.
  • 兵部尚书知青州 - Zhang Qiyan served as the Governor of Qingzhou while holding the title of Minister of War.
  • 梁唐以还 - Refers to the period after the fall of the Tang dynasty, during the Five Dynasties and Ten Kingdoms period (907–979).
  • 洛阳 - Luoyang, a historically significant city and capital of several Chinese dynasties, including the Eastern Han, Western Jin, and Northern Wei.
  • 永乐大典 - The Yongle Dadian, a massive imperial encyclopedic compilation commissioned by the Yongle Emperor of the Ming dynasty (1403–1408).
  • 晁氏读书志 - Book of Reading Records (读书志) by Chao Shi, a Song dynasty scholar and bibliographer.
  • 正史 - The 'official histories' of China, such as the Book of Han, Book of Later Han, Book of Jin, and Song Shi, which were considered authoritative historical records.
  • 别传外传之比 - Refers to works like Biographies of the Immortals or Extravagant Histories, which were considered non-official or supplementary to the zhengshi (正史).
  • 衡阳周令妻报应 - A story involving karmic retribution, a common theme in Chinese folk tales and Buddhist-influenced narratives.
  • 焦生见亡妻 - A story of a man who encounters the ghost of his deceased wife, another common theme in Chinese literature.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - Imperially sanctioned Complete Library of the Four Treasuries.
  • 子部十二 - Twelfth category of the Zibu.
  • 洛阳缙绅旧闻记 - Records of Old Stories from the Gentry of Luoyang.
  • 【臣】等谨案 - We subjects respectfully note.
  • 累官同中书门下平章事 - Accumulated appointments to the position of Chancellor of the Central Secretariat and the Office of the Door of the Palace.
  • 以司空致仕 - Retired as a Grand Works Minister.
  • 卒諡文定 - Died and was posthumously honored with the title 'Wen Ding'.
  • 事迹具宋史本传 - His biography is detailed in the official history of the Song dynasty.
  • 是书前题乙巳岁 - The preface of this book is dated the year of the Earth Snake (乙巳).
  • 真宗景徳二年 - The second year of the Jingde era of Emperor Zhenzong.
  • 兵部尚书知青州 - Served as the Governor of Qingzhou while holding the title of Minister of War.
  • 梁唐以还 - Since the Later Liang and Later Tang dynasties.
  • 凡二十一篇 - A total of twenty-one essays.
  • 书録解题目次与此本合 - The catalog of the book's titles matches this edition.
  • 晁氏读书志 - Chao Shi's Book of Reading Records.
  • 自序明言五卷 - The preface clearly states that it is five volumes.
  • 永乐大典 - The Yongle Dadian.
  • 读书志之误明矣 - The error in Book of Reading Records is clearly evident.
  • 书中多据传说之词 - The book contains many accounts based on legends.
  • 约载事实以为劝诫 - It records facts approximately for the purpose of moral instruction.
  • 自称凡与正史差异者并存而录之 - The author himself states that all accounts differing from the official histories are recorded.
  • 别传外传之比 - Comparable to other biographies and external accounts.
  • 衡阳周令妻报应 - The story of the wife of the magistrate of Hengyang and her karmic retribution.
  • 焦生见亡妻 - Jiao's encounter with his deceased wife.
  • 不免涉于 - Cannot help but involve.

[2]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 語怪 - Refers to discussing or narrating strange or supernatural events, often found in folklore or legends.
  • 非命而死 - Means 'to die an unnatural or premature death,' often implying foul play or misfortune.
  • 憂沮不知其計 - Means 'feeling distressed and unable to think of a plan,' indicating a state of despair or helplessness.
  • 案通鉴五代史 - Refers to consulting the 'Comprehensive Mirror for Aid in Government' (Zizhi Tongjian) and the 'History of the Five Dynasties,' two important historical texts.
  • 東丹 - Refers to Yelü Bei, the eldest son of Emperor Taizu of Liao and elder brother of Emperor Taizong of Liao, who was given the Chinese name 'Zan Hua' after fleeing to the Later Tang.
  • 昭信節度使 - A title given to Yelü Bei, indicating his role as a military governor.
  • 旋即灭亡晋 - Means 'the Later Jin quickly perished,' referring to the rapid decline of the Later Jin dynasty.
  • 得国后为之备礼送归 - Means 'after gaining the throne, he prepared a proper funeral ceremony and sent him back,' indicating the respectful treatment of the deceased.
  • 事隔两朝 - Means 'the event occurred across two dynasties,' emphasizing the temporal distance between the events.
  • 遽形忧沮 - Means 'immediately showed signs of distress,' indicating a sudden and visible reaction to the news.
  • 此事殆出传闻之讹 - Means 'this matter is likely based on erroneous hearsay,' suggesting that the story is unreliable.
  • 五代史阙文 - Refers to the 'History of the Five Dynasties' and its missing or incomplete sections.
  • 乾隆四十六年三月恭校上 - Indicates the date and the person who submitted the text, specifically the third month of the forty-sixth year of the Qianlong era.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 契丹 - Refers to the Khitan people, who established the Liao dynasty in northern China.
  • 降王东丹 - Refers to Yelü Bei, who was forced to flee to the Later Tang after being betrayed by his younger brother.
  • 朝廷密害之非命而死 - Implies that the Later Tang court secretly killed Yelü Bei, which is a significant historical event.
  • 太宗之兄 - Refers to Yelü Bei's relationship as the elder brother of Emperor Taizong of Liao.
  • 奔唐 - Means 'fled to the Tang,' indicating Yelü Bei's escape to the Later Tang.
  • 潞王 - Refers to Li Congke, the last emperor of the Later Tang.
  • 秦继旻 - A宦者 (eunuch) who played a role in the plot against Yelü Bei.
  • 皇城使李彦绅 - A military official who was involved in the plot.
  • 备礼送归 - Indicates the proper funeral rites and the return of the body, showing respect and adherence to tradition.

Proper Nouns

  • 李少师 - Refers to Li Shao, a historical figure.
  • 东丹 - Refers to Yelü Bei.
  • 契丹 - Refers to the Khitan people.
  • 李肃 - A historical figure who was sent to guard the funeral procession.
  • 潞王 - Refers to Li Congke.
  • 秦继旻 - A宦者 (eunuch) involved in the plot.
  • 李彦绅 - A military official involved in the plot.
  • 张全义 - A historical figure known for his contributions to the governance of Luoyang.
  • 五代史 - Refers to the 'History of the Five Dynasties.'

[3]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 总纂官 - Chief editor; responsible for overseeing the compilation of a text.
  • 总校官 - Chief proofreader; responsible for finalizing the text.
  • 洛阳搢绅旧闻记原序 - Preface to the 'Records of Old News in Luoyang'; a scholarly work documenting historical anecdotes.
  • 褒贬陈迹理甚明白使人终日聴之忘倦退而记之旋失其本 - The stories were so clear and well-reasoned that they were enjoyable to listen to, but the author soon forgot them.
  • 无暇著述今眼昬足重率多忘失 - The author had no time to write, and now, due to old age, he has forgotten much.
  • 营丘事有条贯足病累月终朝宴坐无所用心 - The author has been busy with matters in Yingqiu, and has not had the time or energy to focus on writing.
  • 追思曩昔搢绅所説及余亲所见闻得二十余事因编次之分为五卷 - The author has compiled over twenty stories into five volumes.
  • 摭旧老之所説必稽事实约前史之例动求劝诫乡曲小辩略而不书 - The author has included stories that are based on facts, and follows the examples of previous historians, but has omitted trivial disputes.
  • 与正史差异者并存而录之则别传外传比也斯皆搢绅所谈因命之曰洛阳搢绅旧闻记庶可传信览之无惑焉 - The author has included stories that differ from the official histories, and has called the work 'Records of Old News in Luoyang,' hoping that it will be a reliable source of information.
  • 宋朝乙巳嵗夏六月营丘张齐贤序 - The preface was written in the summer of the year 乙巳 (1075) in Yingqiu by Zhang Qixian.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 洛阳 - Luoyang, a major city in Chinese history, particularly during the Eastern Han, Wei, Jin, and Northern and Southern Dynasties periods.
  • 搢绅 - Scholar-officials or members of the gentry class, who played a significant role in Chinese society and governance.
  • 唐梁已还五代间事 - Refers to the Tang Dynasty (618-907) and the Later Liang period (907-923) of the Five Dynasties period (907-960).
  • 营丘 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific location in China.
  • 宋朝乙巳嵗 - The year 1075 in the Song Dynasty, indicated by the sexagenary cycle (乙巳).

Proper Nouns

  • 总纂官【臣】纪昀 - The chief editor, Ji Yun.
  • 总校官【臣】陆费墀 - The chief proofreader, Lu Feichi.
  • 营丘张齐贤 - Zhang Qixian, the author of the preface, from Yingqiu.

[4]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 钦定四库全书 - A massive collection of Chinese literature compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 洛阳搢绅旧闻记 - Records of old news or historical anecdotes about officials and scholars in Luoyang.
  • 英威刚狠 - Describes Zhu Wen as having a fierce and resolute personality.
  • 视之若乳虎 - A metaphor comparing Zhu Wen to a tiger nursing its cubs, implying he was both fierce and protective.
  • 左右小忤其旨立杀之 - Describes Zhu Wen's extreme intolerance for any slight disobedience, leading to immediate execution.
  • 归必相贺 - Upon returning home, officials would greet each other with congratulations, likely to avoid suspicion.
  • 进士杜荀鹤以所业投之且乞一见 - Describes Du Xunhe, a scholar, submitting his work to Zhu Wen and requesting an audience.
  • 掌客以事闻于梁祖 - The official in charge of guests reported the matter to Zhu Wen.
  • 荀鹤住大梁数月 - Du Xunhe stayed in Daliang (the capital of the Later Liang) for several months.
  • 凡有求谒梁祖如已通姓名而未得见者 - Describes anyone who had been introduced to Zhu Wen but had not yet met him.
  • 虽逾年困踬于逆旅中寒饿殊甚 - Describes the harsh conditions faced by those who had not yet met Zhu Wen, often staying in inns for years and suffering from cold and hunger.
  • 主者留之不令私去 - The officials in charge would not allow them to leave privately.
  • 不尔即公人辈及祸矣 - If they tried to leave on their own, they would face severe consequences.
  • 荀鹤逐日诣客次 - Du Xunhe visited the guest quarters daily.
  • 一旦梁祖在便防谓左右曰杜荀鹤何在 - One day, Zhu Wen, while in his private quarters, asked where Du Xunhe was.
  • 左右以见在客次为对 - The officials replied that Du Xunhe was in the guest quarters.
  • 未几忽有驰骑至者 - Soon after, a messenger arrived on horseback.
  • 梁祖见之至已午间方退 - Zhu Wen saw the messenger and did not leave until noon.
  • 梁祖遽起归宅 - Zhu Wen suddenly got up and returned home.
  • 荀鹤谓掌客者曰某饥甚告欲归 - Du Xunhe told the official in charge that he was very hungry and wanted to return home.
  • 公人辈为设食且曰乞命若大王出要见秀才言已归馆舎即某等求死 - The officials provided him with food and said that if Zhu Wen came out to see the scholar, they would all be in danger of death.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁太祖 - Zhu Wen, the founder of the Later Liang dynasty, who ruled from 907 to 912.
  • 杜荀鹤 - A renowned poet and scholar of the late Tang and early Five Dynasties period.
  • 大梁 - The capital of the Later Liang dynasty, located in modern-day Kaifeng, Henan.
  • 逆旅 - Refers to inns or temporary lodging places, often used to describe the precarious living conditions of officials and scholars.

Proper Nouns

  • 钦定四库全书 - The 'Four Treasuries,' a massive collection of Chinese literature compiled during the Qing dynasty.
  • 洛阳搢绅旧闻记 - Records of old news of the officials and scholars of Luoyang.
  • 梁太祖 - Zhu Wen, the founder of the Later Liang dynasty.
  • 杜荀鹤 - A renowned poet and scholar of the late Tang and early Five Dynasties period.
  • 大梁 - The capital of the Later Liang dynasty, located in modern-day Kaifeng, Henan.
  • 逆旅 - Inns or temporary lodging places.

[5]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 不暇至申未间 - A phrase indicating that there was no time to reach the period between the hours of Shen (申) and Wei (未), which corresponds roughly to 3 PM to 5 PM.
  • 梁祖果出 - Indicates that Emperor Liang Zhu (梁祖) indeed came out.
  • 令取骰子来 - An order to bring dice.
  • 掷且久终不惬旨 - The act of throwing lasted a long time without satisfying the emperor's intention.
  • 怒甚屡顾左右 - The emperor became very angry and repeatedly looked around at his attendants.
  • 左右怖惧缩颈重足蹈汤火 - The attendants were terrified, shrinking their necks and stepping heavily as if walking on hot coals.
  • 命坐荀鹤 - Ordered Du Xunhe (杜荀鹤) to sit.
  • 似有雨防 - There seemed to be a rain shelter.
  • 下令视之实雨也 - Ordered to look and it was indeed raining.
  • 仰首视之天无片云雨防甚大霑陛檐有声 - Looking up, there was no cloud in the sky, but the rain shelter was heavily drenched and made a sound.
  • 秀才曽见无云雨否 - Have you ever seen rain without clouds?
  • 答言未曾见 - Answered that he had never seen such a thing.
  • 笑 - The emperor laughed.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁祖 - Refers to Emperor Liang Zhu (梁祖), the founder of the Later Liang dynasty.
  • 杜荀鹤 - A famous poet and scholar of the Tang dynasty.
  • 骰子 - Dice, used for gambling and divination.
  • 秀才 - A term for scholars or literati, often used to address someone with high social status or education.
  • 雨防 - A rain shelter or canopy.
  • 汤火 - Metaphorically refers to a dangerous situation, like walking on hot coals.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence structure is compact, indicating that there was no time to reach a certain hour, and then the emperor came out.
  • The sequence of actions is elliptical: the emperor ordered to bring dice, then threw them, indicating some form of divination, which lasted a long time without satisfying his intention.
  • The sentence describes the emperor's anger and the attendants' fear in a vivid manner.
  • The sequence of actions is elliptical: the emperor ordered Du Xunhe to sit, the host introduced him, and he was hurriedly brought to the steps.
  • Du Xunhe was not supposed to rush up the steps.
  • The sequence of actions is elliptical: the emperor noticed a rain shelter, ordered to look, and it was indeed raining, despite the clear sky.

Proper Nouns

  • 梁祖 - Emperor Liang Zhu (梁祖).
  • 杜荀鹤 - Du Xunhe (杜荀鹤).

[7]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 争表梁王造化功 - A phrase indicating a request to present a memorial to Li Keyong, praising his achievements. '争表' means 'to present a memorial,' and '造化功' means 'achievements of creation' or 'great accomplishments.'
  • 頓忘其病 - '頓忘' means 'suddenly forgot' or 'temporarily forgot,' and '其病' refers to his illness.
  • 趨步如飛 - A metaphorical expression meaning 'to move quickly' or 'to walk briskly.'
  • 連拜敘謝數四 - '連拜' means 'to bow repeatedly,' and '敘謝' means 'to express gratitude.' '數四' indicates that this was done multiple times.
  • 特帳設賔館 - '特帳' means 'a special tent,' and '賔館' means 'guesthouse' or 'inn.' This phrase indicates that Li Keyong provided a special guesthouse for Du Mu.
  • 賜之衣服錢物 - '賜之' means 'gave him,' and '衣服錢物' means 'clothes and money.'
  • 一字奉絹一疋 - One word is rewarded with one bolt of silk.
  • 目覩辭多不載 - Many of the words were not recorded.
  • 遂留於賔館厚禮待之 - He was then kept in the guesthouse and treated with great respect.
  • 任是秦皇漢武 - Even if it were Qin Shi Huang or Han Wu Di.
  • 任是秦皇漢武不死何歸 - Even if Qin Shi Huang or Han Wu Di did not die, where would they go?
  • 憾其有此深切之句爾 - He was upset by these deep and poignant lines.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁祖 - Li Keyong (李克用), a powerful warlord during the late Tang dynasty, known for his military prowess and political influence.
  • 杜秀才 - Du Mu (杜牧), a renowned poet and scholar of the Tang dynasty, known for his poetry and political commentary.
  • 徐夤 - Xu Yin (徐夤), a poet and scholar from Fujian, known for his literary works.
  • 秦皇漢武 - Qin Shi Huang (秦始皇) and Han Wu Di (漢武帝), two of the most famous emperors in Chinese history, known for their military conquests and political reforms.

Proper Nouns

  • 梁祖 - Li Keyong (李克用)
  • 杜秀才 - Du Mu (杜牧)
  • 徐夤 - Xu Yin (徐夤)
  • 秦皇 - Qin Shi Huang (秦始皇)
  • 漢武 - Han Wu Di (漢武帝)
  • 王喬 - Wang Qiao (王喬)

[8]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 憇于髙栁树下 - Resting under a tall willow tree, where '憇' means to rest or take a break.
  • 树可数围 - Describes a tree that is several times around in circumference, emphasizing its size.
  • 柯甚大 - Indicates that the branches are very large, with '柯' referring to branches and '' indicating largeness.
  • 可庇五六十人 - Means the tree can provide shelter for fifty or sixty people.
  • 坐客各各避席对曰 - Means 'the seated guests each got up and replied,' where '各各' means each and every one, '避席' means to get up from one's seat, and '对曰' means to reply.
  • 好作车头 - Means 'good for making the head of a carriage,' where '作' means to make and '车头' means the head of a carriage.
  • 末坐五六人起对 - Means 'a few people sitting at the end got up and replied,' where '末坐' means sitting at the end, '五六人' means a few people, and '起对' means got up and replied.
  • 虽好栁树作车头须是夹榆树 - Means 'even if the willow tree is good for making the head of a carriage, it must be paired with elm trees,' where '虽' means even if, '须是' means must be, and '夹榆树' means paired with elm trees.
  • 这一队措大爱顺口弄人 - Means 'these scholars love to flatter people with smooth talk,' where '措大' refers to scholars or literati, '爱' means love, '顺口' means smooth talk, and '弄人' means to flatter people.
  • 有甚难事 - Means 'what is difficult about it,' where '有甚' means what is and '难事' means difficult things.
  • 更待甚须臾 - Means 'wait a little while longer,' where '更待' means wait and '须臾' means a short time.
  • 悉擒言柳树好作车头者数以谀佞之罪当面扑杀之 - Means 'all those who said the willow tree is good for making the head of a carriage were caught and killed on the spot for flattery,' where '悉擒' means all were caught, '言柳树好作车头者' means those who said the willow tree is good for making the head of a carriage, '数以谀佞之罪' means for the crime of flattery, and '当面扑杀之' means were killed on the spot.
  • 刚猛英断以权数御物遂成兴王之业岂偶然哉 - Means 'his boldness, decisiveness, and use of strategy to control others led to the establishment of his dynasty, which was not accidental,' where '刚猛英断' means boldness and decisiveness, '以权数御物' means using strategy to control others, '遂成兴王之业' means led to the establishment of his dynasty, and '岂偶然哉' means which was not accidental.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 梁祖 - Refers to Li Keyong (李克用), a prominent warlord and founder of the Later Liang dynasty during the late Tang period.

  • 游客 - Refers to visitors or travelers.

  • 兴王之业 - Refers to the establishment of a dynasty, specifically the Later Liang dynasty founded by Li Keyong.
  • 权数御物 - Refers to the use of strategy and power to control others, a common theme in political and military leadership.
  • 谀佞 - Refers to flattery and sycophancy, which were often seen as negative traits in Chinese political culture.
  • 扑杀 - Refers to killing on the spot, a harsh punishment that reflects the severity of Li Keyong's rule.

  • 秦时指鹿为马 - A historical allusion to the story of Zhao Gao (赵高) during the Qin dynasty, where he famously claimed that a deer was a horse to test the loyalty of his subordinates.

  • 即旧老之言可验矣 - Classical structure meaning 'this confirms the old sayings' or 'this proves the old people's words.'
  • 王始至洛 - 'The King first arrived at Luoyang.' '王' refers to a ruler or king, likely a military leader or local governor.
  • 于麾下百人中选可使者一十八人 - 'From among his hundred subordinates, he selected eighteen who could be sent.' Complex clause with implied subject and verb.
  • 命之曰屯将 - 'He appointed them as tun jiang (屯将).' Title likely meaning 'commander of a garrison' or 'military commander in charge of a settlement.'
  • 每人给旗一口榜一道 - 'Each person was given one flag and one notice.' '榜' here is likely a notice or proclamation.
  • 令招农户令自耕种 - 'He ordered them to recruit farmers and let them cultivate the land themselves.' Repetition of '令' is a classical stylistic feature.
  • 流民渐归 - 'The displaced people gradually returned.' '流民' refers to displaced or wandering peasants.
  • 王于百人中又选可使者十八人命之曰屯副 - 'The King again selected eighteen from the hundred and appointed them as tun fu (屯副).' *屯副* is likely the deputy or assistant to the *tun jiang*.
  • 民之来者绥抚之 - 'For the people who came, he pacified and comforted them.' '绥抚' means to pacify or comfort.

  • 王又麾下选书计一十八人命之曰屯判官 - 'The King again selected eighteen from his ranks and appointed them as tun pan guan (屯判官).' Likely means 'garrison judge' or 'military administrator.'
  • 农隙毎选丁夫教以弓矢鎗劒 - 'During the farming off-season, he selected able-bodied men and taught them the use of bows, arrows, spears, and swords.' *农隙* is a classical term for the period when farming is not in season.

  • 共得丁夫闲弓矢鎗劒者二万余人 - 'In total, there were over twenty thousand able-bodied men trained in bows, arrows, spears, and swords.' *丁夫* refers to adult males or able-bodied men.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '寛事简逺近归之如市' must be read as a single unit, indicating that the king's policies of simplification attracted people from far and near.
  • The rhetorical question '乱后易治乎抑王得简易之道乎' must be read as a choice between two possibilities: whether it is easier to govern after chaos or if the king found a simple way to govern.
  • The long sequence '教习之时李罕之在河阳罕之奸贼也常破北山之摩云寨当时号为李摩云亦尝置寨于洛城中至今民呼其寨地为李摩云寨寨之西号寨西市时罕之镇三城知王专以教民耕织为务常宣言于众曰田舍翁何足惮王闻之蔑如也' must be read as a single unit, describing Li Han's activities and the king's response.
  • The long sequence '尺书于王求军食及缣帛王曰李太傅所要不得不奉之左右及賔席咸以为不可与王曰苐与之似若畏之者左右不之晓罕之谓王畏己不设备因罕之举兵怀泽王乃密召屯兵潜师夜发迟明入三城罕之顾无归路遂逃遁投' must be read as a single unit, describing the exchange between Li Taifu and the king, and the subsequent military action.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 太尉处吕曰 - Formal address, where '太尉' is a high-ranking official, and '处吕' is likely a name or title. '曰' indicates that what follows is a statement.
  • 某闻之乆矣 - '某' is a humble self-reference, '闻之' means 'heard of,' and '乆矣' means 'for a long time.' Together, it translates to 'I have heard of this for a long time.'
  • 太尉武人却能如此好道 - '武人' refers to a military person, '却' indicates contrast, and '好道' means 'loves the Dao' or 'is devoted to the Dao.' This phrase suggests that a military person can be devoted to the Dao.
  • 此人有寿今已有防疾矣 - '此人' means 'this person,' '有寿' means 'has a long life,' '今已' means 'now already,' and '防疾' means 'sickness' or 'illness.' Together, it translates to 'this person has a long life but now already has sickness.'
  • 某当暂去与少药疗之 - '某' is a humble self-reference, '当暂去' means 'will temporarily leave,' '与' means 'to bring,' '少药' means 'a little medicine,' and '疗之' means 'to treat it.' Together, it translates to 'I will temporarily leave and bring a little medicine to treat it.'
  • 田闻言大喜曰 - '田' is a name, '闻言' means 'heard the words,' '大喜' means 'greatly delighted,' and '曰' indicates that what follows is a statement. Together, it translates to 'Tian heard the words and was greatly delighted, saying.'
  • 重进麤人何消神仙下降 - '重进' is a name, '麤人' means 'a rough person,' '何消' means 'how could there be a need,' and '神仙下降' means 'gods descending.' Together, it translates to 'Zhongjin, a rough person, how could there be a need for gods to descend?'
  • 此月十五日夜三更必至 - '此月' means 'this month,' '十五日' means 'the fifteenth day,' '夜三更' means 'midnight,' and '必至' means 'will surely come.' Together, it translates to 'this month on the fifteenth day at midnight will surely come.'
  • 太尉自斋沐换新衣具靴笏深夜之必来降矣 - '太尉' is a high-ranking official, '自斋沐' means 'to purify oneself,' '换新衣' means 'change into new clothes,' '具靴笏' means 'prepare boots and a tablet,' '深夜之' means 'wait for it at midnight,' and '必来降矣' means 'will surely come down.' Together, it translates to 'the Taifu will purify himself, change into new clothes, prepare boots and a tablet, and wait for it at midnight, it will surely come down.'
  • 重进曰谨受教至期命陈设东位帷帐裀榻一一新洁焚香燃烛斋洁披秉瞻望星斗拜告以俟其至须臾报三更矣不至又取香燃之望空再拜时重进足重兼染风恙甚难折腰是夕拜讫大喘流汗衣皆霑湿略无倦怠须臾又报四更重进虽燃 - '重进曰' means 'Zhongjin said,' '谨受教' means 'humbly accept the instruction,' '至期' means 'by the appointed time,' '命陈设东位帷帐裀榻' means 'order the arrangement of the eastern position with curtains, beds, and mats,' '一一新洁' means 'all new and clean,' '焚香燃烛' means 'burn incense and light candles,' '斋洁' means 'purify,' '披秉' means 'wear and hold,' '瞻望星斗' means 'look up at the stars,' '拜告' means 'bow and inform,' '以俟其至' means 'to wait for its arrival,' '须臾报三更矣' means 'soon it was reported that it was midnight,' '不至' means 'did not come,' '又取香燃之' means 'again take incense and burn it,' '望空再拜' means 'bow to the sky again,' '时重进足重兼染风恙甚难折腰' means 'at that time, Zhongjin was heavy-footed and also had a wind illness, making it very difficult to bow,' '是夕拜讫大喘流汗衣皆霑湿略无倦怠' means 'that night, after bowing, he was panting, sweating, and his clothes were all wet, but he showed no sign of fatigue,' '须臾又报四更重进虽燃' means 'soon it was reported that it was the fourth watch, and Zhongjin was still burning incense.'

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 香未辍意疑讶引颈瞻望略无兆朕 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that the incense continued to burn while the mind was filled with suspicion and astonishment, and there were no signs or omens.
  • 报四更五防重进疑怪殊甚 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that during the fifth watch of the night, Heavy Advance was greatly suspicious and strange.
  • 问花项等三人欲责其虚诞 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that Heavy Advance questioned three people, including Flower Neck, and wanted to blame them for being false or exaggerated.
  • 亲信人来白尊师门大开中并无人向来囊箧般运已尽 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that a trusted person came to report that the master's door was wide open, there was no one inside, and the bags and chests had been moved away completely.
  • 盖花项等诳令开东边便门揭箧俱潜遁矣 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that it was because Flower Neck and others deceived him into opening the eastern side gate, and they took the chests and all fled secretly.
  • 重进慙恨嗟叹但鸣指谓左右曰无良汉无良汉自是无复求道术矣 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that Heavy Advance was ashamed, resentful, and sighed, and just pointed and said to those around him, 'bad men, bad men,' and from then on, he no longer sought Daoist arts.
  • 时永兴有匿名人遗诗二首嘲之置诗于防事前田命賔席读之愈惭乃散差人追捕皆不获诗本失其一前永兴士人多能诵之余授右仆射判永兴军备知其事录之以诫贪夫云 - This phrase is a bit ambiguous, but it suggests that at that time, a hidden person in Yongxing left two poems to mock him, placed the poems in front of the guard station, Tian ordered the guests to read them, he was even more ashamed and then dispersed, he sent people to chase and capture them, but they were not caught, one of the poems was lost, many people in Yongxing could recite them, and I, as the Right Vice-Chancellor and Judge of Yongxing's military affairs, recorded this to warn greedy men.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This long sentence must be read as a single action: 'He then told the master's wife that he had no legitimate wife and took this woman as his wife, treating her with the same courtesy as if she were a wife.'
  • This long sequence must be read as a series of events: 'When he was appointed governor of Yuzhou, he traveled with her, and all the private property belonged to Yanjun, but he already had many concubines and grew tired of her. He planned to return to the capital from Yuzhou, killed her while drunk, buried her behind the governor's office in Yuzhou, and traveled by boat down the river from Xiangxi. In the evening, he saw a small boat with several women approaching his boat. He looked closely at them, and they were the wife he had killed in Yuzhou. She was dressed in bright, new clothes, hit him, and cursed him, saying, 'You took my whole family, you took my money and valuables, after you took me as your wife, you dug up everything in my family's property, not leaving a single thing. I did nothing wrong to you, why did you kill me? I have already appealed to heaven, and I will get my life back.' Her voice was very loud, and everyone on the boat heard her. Soon she disappeared.'
  • This long sequence must be read as a series of actions: 'He was terrified and promised to donate to monks and perform good deeds. From then on, he saw her every night as before, until the night he arrived at Jingzhou, he did not see her. When he landed in Song, he first went to a temple, donated money to set up a feast, and performed good deeds.'

I am not a human but a ghost. You killed me, and the villagers discarded my body in a half-mile distant dry well. Now it is filled, but there is still a slight well shape. My bones are still there, and I have no place to be reborn.'

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 偕往五年不知信耗 - Accompanied for five years, no news or word of their condition. '信耗' refers to news or word, often used in the context of someone's condition or whereabouts.
  • 父母忧之 - '忧之' is a classical expression meaning 'to be concerned about.'
  • 一旦归已儒服矣 - One day, upon returning, he was already in the robes of a scholar. This is a metaphor for his transformation into a learned person.
  • 昼夜勤苦 - Diligently working day and night. A common expression to describe intense study or labor.
  • 通大小经 - To understand both major and minor classics. Refers to mastery of both major and minor Confucian texts.
  • 臻圣人阃奥 - To reach the innermost depths of the sages. '阃奥' is a metaphor for the profound and hidden knowledge of sages.
  • 尤善书札有体法 - Especially skilled in writing letters with proper form and style. '书札' refers to letters or written correspondence.
  • 数年善词赋诗篇 - After several years, he became skilled in writing poetry and prose. '词赋诗篇' refers to literary forms such as poetry and prose.
  • 乡党推服 - The local people revered and respected him. '乡党' refers to the local community or fellow townspeople.
  • 遂成通儒焉 - Thus he became a learned scholar. '通儒' refers to a well-rounded and highly educated scholar.
  • 同人日造其门 - Scholars and peers visited his door daily. '同人' refers to fellow scholars or people in the same field.
  • 声价籍甚 - His reputation was greatly esteemed. A classical expression for high reputation or prestige.
  • 郡人苦之 - The people of the county suffered from it. '苦之' is a classical expression meaning 'to suffer from it.'
  • 郡主亦知之未能去 - The governor also knew about it but could not remove the corrupt officials. '未能去' means 'could not remove' or 'could not get rid of.'
  • 适有郡长故人衔命过其郡者亦颇闻之 - Just then, a former friend of the governor, who was on an official mission, passed through the county and also heard about it. This is an elliptical and complex clause.
  • 且惧朝廷知之 - And they feared that the imperial court would learn of it. '惧' is a classical verb meaning 'to fear.'
  • 过客曰莫如请一人正直賔席即郡事必治公之左右亦悛改矣 - The visitor said, 'It would be best to invite one upright person to serve as a guest official; then the affairs of the county would be properly managed, and the governor’s attendants would also reform themselves.' This is a complex sentence with a conditional structure.
  • 郡长屡访之未得其人 - The governor searched for such a person many times but could not find him. '屡访之' means 'sought after repeatedly.'
  • 或有言大监之名者 - Someone mentioned the name of the Great Supervisor (大监). This is a title, possibly of a high official or scholar.
  • 大监但以书启致谢托以读书因患肺疾惧不任事为辞 - The Great Supervisor only replied with a letter of thanks, excusing himself by citing his illness and fear of being unable to take on the responsibilities. This is a complex sentence with a polite refusal.
  • 郡主讶之曰张秀才贫乏如是某已简牒服玩鞍马请之 - The governor was surprised and said, 'This Zhang scholar is so poor that I have already sent documents, clothing, and horse gear to invite him.' This is a dialogue with a complex structure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 儒服 - Scholar’s robes. Refers to the traditional clothing worn by Confucian scholars, symbolizing their status and learning.
  • 大小经 - Major and minor classics. Refers to the Confucian canon, including the Five Classics and other texts.
  • 圣人阃奥 - The innermost knowledge of the sages. A metaphor for the deepest philosophical or moral insights.
  • 书札有体法 - Letters with proper form and style. Refers to the classical Chinese art of letter-writing with strict rules of form and decorum.
  • 词赋诗篇 - Poetry and prose. Refers to the literary forms of the time, including regulated verse and prose essays.
  • 乡党 - Local community. A term from Confucian philosophy, referring to the people of one's hometown or locality.
  • 通儒 - A learned scholar. A term for someone who has mastered the classical texts and is well-versed in philosophy, literature, and governance.
  • 绛台 - Likely a place name or residence, possibly in the region of Jiangzhou.
  • 同人 - Fellow scholars. A term used in classical Chinese to refer to people in the same field of study or profession.
  • 声价籍甚 - Great reputation. A classical expression for high prestige or esteem.
  • 绛州 - A historical county or region, likely in modern-day Shanxi Province.

  • 大监 - Great Supervisor. A title that may refer to a high-ranking official or scholar, possibly a mentor or influential figure.
  • 张秀才 - Zhang, the scholar. A respectful term for a young scholar or student.
  • 简牒 - Official documents or summons. Refers to formal documents used to invite or appoint someone.
  • 服玩鞍马 - Clothing, furnishings, and horse gear. A list of gifts or provisions offered to someone in honor or invitation.

  • 复泣而告曰 - Means 'again wept and said.'

  • 前拜告皆弗聴 - Means 'before, when she was asked, she did not listen.'

  • 由是所弹事中寝焉 - Means 'therefore, the matter of the impeachment was quietly dropped.'
  • 大监曰安有教化之地泄人弹辞使来相告 - Means 'the Grand Supervisor said, 'How can there be a place of moral instruction where one leaks the content of a memorial to have it conveyed to the other party?'
  • 然吾老矣遂有山林长往之心焉 - Means 'but I am old, and I have long had the intention of retreating to the mountains and forests.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 滑净光莹玉者 - Describes a stone that is smooth, clean, glossy, and resembles jade.  is a variant of 似, meaning 'like' or 'resembling.'

  • 乗闲于洛濵 - 'To take leisure time by the banks of the Luo River.' 濱 is a variant of 滨 (bank).
  • 选拣白石为翫物 - 'To select white stones as playthings.' 翆 is a variant of 玩 (play, toy).
  • 光洁温润 - 'Smooth, clean, warm, and smooth.' Describes the physical qualities of the stone.
  • 可解治之乎 - 'Can it be cut and processed?' A question asking whether the stone can be worked or carved.
  • 沉吟久之 - 'Murmured for a long time.' Indicates hesitation or deep thought.
  • 谓之为石即重而且润谓之为玉又外状不 - Complex clause: 'If it is called a stone, it is heavy and smooth; if it is called jade, its external form is not like jade.'
  • 试治之 - 'Try to process it.' Refers to attempting to cut or carve the stone.
  • 解玉砂 - A specialized tool used for cutting jade.
  • 截五之一焉 - 'Cut off one fifth of it.' Refers to the portion of the stone that was cut.
  • 中心空虚有物在其内微动 - 'The center is hollow, and there is something inside that moves slightly.' Indicates the presence of a living creature inside the stone.
  • 小白也 - 'It is a small white turtle.' Again,  is a variant of 龟 (turtle).
  • 骧首引殻犹犹而行 - 'Raised its head, pulled its shell, and moved slowly.' Describes the turtle’s movement.
  • 石既混成周无隙 - 'The stone is a single mass with no gaps.' Indicates that the stone is solid and unbroken, yet it contained a living creature inside.

  • 中书 - Refers to the Central Secretariat, a high-level administrative office.
  • 玉工 - Refers to jade workers or artisans.
  • 神物 - Refers to a divine or supernatural object.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '罅即是也从何而入' is somewhat ambiguous. It could mean 'the gap is the turtle, from where does it enter?' or 'the gap is the turtle, and it enters from where?'
  • The sentence '李取漆合贮之日于佛前燃香供养之人知求观者甚众' is a bit convoluted. It could be interpreted as 'Li took lacquer to store it together, and daily before the Buddha, burning incense for worship, people know that those who seek to see are very numerous.'
  • The sentence '李命数人同送于洛濵去水三数步放之甲不动食顷引首左顾向水而去' describes a sequence of actions but is somewhat difficult to parse. It could be broken down into: 'Li ordered several people to send it together to the Luoyang bank, three or several steps away from the water, released it, the turtle shell did not move, after a meal, it raised its head, looked to the left, and went towards the water.'
  • The sentence '及入水不没履水逆行约数丈渐没入水遂不复见' describes the turtle's behavior in water but is somewhat complex. It could be broken down into: 'when it entered the water, it did not sink, walked on the water, went backward for about several zhang, gradually submerged into the water and was no longer seen.'
  • The sentence '龙耶耶众不之测' is a question of identification. It could be interpreted as 'was it a dragon or a turtle? The crowd could not measure it.'

Proper Nouns

  • 太宗 - Refers to Emperor Taizong of Tang.
  • 赵韩王普 - Refers to Zhao Pu, a prominent official during the reign of Emperor Taizong.
  • 中书 - Refers to the Central Secretariat.
  • 玉工 - Refers to jade workers or artisans.

[103]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 大通舟檝之利 - Literal phrase meaning 'greatly facilitating the benefits of boats and oars'; refers to improved waterway transportation.
  • 亦无他异 - Literal phrase meaning 'there were no other unusual occurrences'; indicates that nothing else out of the ordinary happened.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 嘉猷坊 - Specific location, likely a place name in the region, transliterated as 'Jiayou Fang'.
  • 巩 - Place name, likely referring to Gong County, a historical county in Henan Province.
  • 黄河 - The Yellow River, one of the major rivers in China.
  • 晋司空 - High-ranking official during the Jin Dynasty, equivalent to a Prime Minister.
  • 汉书 - The *Book of Han*, a historical text compiled during the Eastern Han Dynasty.
  • 黄门将作使 - High-ranking official responsible for construction and labor supervision during the Tang Dynasty.
  • 赵【失其名】 - Name that is lost, indicating that the specific individual's name is unknown.

Proper Nouns

  • 嘉猷坊 - Transliterated as 'Jiayou Fang'.
  • 巩 - Transliterated as 'Gong'.
  • 黄河 - Transliterated as 'Yellow River'.
  • 晋司空 - Transliterated as 'Jin Prime Minister'.
  • 汉书 - Transliterated as 'Book of Han'.
  • 黄门将作使 - Transliterated as 'Yellow Gate Supervisor'.
  • 赵【失其名】 - Transliterated as 'Zhao [name lost]'.
